rulings.Case Title: MINDY FREILE VS. TIM LINCECUM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Freile'S Motion For Reconsideration Of The Court'S July 26, 2013, Discovery Order
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 26, 2013, line 1. Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Freile's Motion For Reconsideration Of The Court's July 26, 2013, Discovery Order DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain its burden under CCP 1008. Sanctions granted the amount of $3,150 to be paid to Defendant's counsel within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Prevailing Party to Prepare an Order. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MINDY FREILE VS. TIM LINCECUM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Terminating Sanctions, Or, In The Alternative, Issue Sanctions Based On Pltf'S Failure To Comply With This Court'S Jul 26, 2013 Discovery Order
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 26, 2013, line 1. DEFENDANT TIM LINCECUM's Motion For Terminating Sanctions, Or, In The Alternative, Issue Sanctions Based On Plaintiff's Failure To Comply With This Court's July 26, 2013 Discovery Order DENIED in part and GRANTED in part. Motion is DENIED as to Terminating Sanctions, and Granted as to Issue sanctions. No opposition filed. Plaintiff's objections are overruled. SFLR 8.10 provides in relevant part that "breach of lease" matters are heard in Department 501 "including discovery not handled via pro tempore system," i.e. the motion was properly calendared for Department 302 and transferred to Department 501 when parties failed to stipulate to pro tempore judge hearing the matter. Prevailing Party to Prepare an Order. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL VS. A.H. REALTY, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order For Prejudgment Possession
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 26, 2013, line 2. PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion For Order For Prejudgment Possession Hearing Required to address compensation for installation, monitoring, repair, and maintenance of settlement monitor markers and equipment. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: KAREN LUM VS. JUDY KENT et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 26, 2013, line 3. CROSS DEFENDANT KAREN LUM's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND to (1) allege the creation of the tenancy by "written or oral agreement, sub-tenancy approved by the landlord, or by sufferance" SFRO 37.2(f); and (2) to allege facts regarding severe emotional distress. Demurrer on all other grounds is overruled. Cross-Complainant shall have 10 days from the Notice of Entry of Order to file an amended cross-complaint. Prevailing Party to Prepare an Order. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA VANCIL VS. SAM QUON PON et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 26, 2013, line 9. DEFENDANT SAM PON and SUSAN PON's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OFF CALENDAR. Defendants and their counsel failed to comply with SFLR 2.6C and the Court's prior orders ordering such compliance. See CGC-13-529899 June 19, 2013 minute order and CGC-13-531855 August 5, 2013 tentative ruling. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DIANE LA ROSA et al VS. URAVI LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 26, 2013, line 4. DEFENDANT BALWANTSINH THAKOR's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND as to all causes of action to allege either (1) facts showing agency and/or alter ego; or (2) specific wrongful acts or failure to act by the moving defendant. Demurrer to the cause of action for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress on the ground that that no intent is alleged is OVERRULED, intent/reckless disregard is sufficiently alleged provided P can cure the deficiency addressed above. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DMG ASSET MANAGEMENT, LLC VS. ALCIDES VALLADARES PERLA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Reconsider Motion To Quash Writ Of Possession Exept As To Defendants Named In The Judgment; Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 26, 2013, line 5. DEFENDANT JUAN GALINDO and RENE CASTRO's Motion To Reconsider Motion To Quash Writ Of Possession Exept As To Defendants Named In The Judgment DENIED. Moving parties failed to sustain burden under CCP 1008 and show that information regarding their tenancy (move-in dates, rent amount, etc) was not known or available to them at the time of the original hearing. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SAN MATEO LAND EXCHANGE VS. ALLAN V. PINEDA DDS et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 26, 2013, line 6. PLAINTIFF SAN MATEO LAND EXCHANGE's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED, subject to opposition. = (501/REQ)
